2:33 AM
Got it — so when you manually triggered it, you got a permission/enable prompt instead of the camera view. That's a critical clue.

This points to one of two things:

Camera permission not granted — CAMERA permission missing or denied at runtime
Overlay permission not granted — SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW not granted, so the floating camera view can't draw over DoorDash

1. Check camera permission status:

bash

"Allow GigLens to take pictures and record video?" → Camera permission
"Allow displaying over other apps?" → Overlay permission
"GigLens needs camera access in Settings" → Permission permanently denied
That message text will tell us exactly which gate is failing before we dig into code.

2:34 AM
Found it. Two problems, both confirmed by the output:

Root Cause #1 — SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW not granted at install level
android.permission.SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW: granted=false, flags=[ USER_SET]
The overlay permission was manually denied at some point. Even though appops shows SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW: allow, the install-level granted=false means the app can't draw over other apps reliably on Android 16.

Root Cause #2 — PROJECT_MEDIA rejected
PROJECT_MEDIA: ignore; rejectTime=+38m26s348ms ago
MediaProjection was rejected 38 minutes ago. This means ScreenCaptureService attempted to capture and was denied — so even if the overlay fires, there's no screen capture happening to feed the camera.

Fix Plan
Step 1 — Force-grant SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW:

bash
adb -s 10.0.0.110:43013 shell appops set com.augusteenterprise.giglens SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW allow
Step 2 — Revoke and re-grant MediaProjection cleanly:

bash
adb -s 10.0.0.110:43013 shell appops set com.augusteenterprise.giglens PROJECT_MEDIA allow
Step 3 — Verify both took:

bash
adb -s 10.0.0.110:43013 shell appops get com.augusteenterprise.giglens | grep -E "SYSTEM_ALERT|PROJECT_MEDIA"
Step 4 — Force stop and relaunch to pick up the new grants:

bash
adb -s 10.0.0.110:43013 shell am force-stop com.augusteenterprise.giglens
adb -s 10.0.0.110:43013 shell am start -n com.augusteenterprise.giglens/.MainActivity
Run those four steps, then trigger a test offer and check if the camera pill appears. Paste the Step 3 output so we can confirm the grants before you test.

2:41 AM
The overlay state machine is broken — the pill isn't responding to SHOW_CAMERA at all. Let me look at the code:

bash
cat ~/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/service/OfferOverlayService.kt
Paste the full output and I'll find exactly where the state transition is failing.


package com.augusteenterprise.giglens.service // Author: Claude (Anthropic) - Feature #8: Removed blink — static countdown timer // Persistent floating pill widget. Always visible when enabled in Settings. // Pill + drawer render as one draggable unit via WindowManager. // States: IDLE → PILL(

pasted

2:42 AM
Found the bug. It's in onStartCommand. Look at this block:

kotlin
// Check if ScreenCaptureService died mid-shift
if (!ScreenCaptureService.isRunning &&
    captureWasEverRunning &&
    sheetState != SheetState.CAPTURE_DEAD) {
    sheetState = SheetState.CAPTURE_DEAD
    ...
}
This check runs before the when (intent?.action) block. So when ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA arrives, if ScreenCaptureService.isRunning is false at that moment — even briefly — the service hijacks the state to CAPTURE_DEAD and never reaches the ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA branch.

The fix is to move the CAPTURE_DEAD check inside the else branch so it only fires when there's no actionable intent.

Here's the corrected onStartCommand:

kotlin
override fun onStartCommand(intent: Intent?, flags: Int, startId: Int): Int {
    when (intent?.action) {
        ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA -> {
            captureWasEverRunning = true
            cancelRevertTimer()
            sheetState = SheetState.CAMERA
            if (isViewAdded) updateWidget() else { showWidget(); updateWidget() }
            Log.d(TAG, "Widget morphed to CAMERA state")
            stopSelf(startId)
        }
        ACTION_HIDE_CAMERA -> {
            if (sheetState == SheetState.CAMERA || sheetState == SheetState.PROCESSING) {
                sheetState = if (verdict == "UNKNOWN") SheetState.IDLE else SheetState.PILL
                if (isViewAdded) updateWidget() else { showWidget(); updateWidget() }
                Log.d(TAG, "Widget morphed back from CAMERA to $sheetState")
            }
            stopSelf(startId)
        }
        else -> {
            // CORRECT: only check CAPTURE_DEAD when no actionable intent — not before ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA
            // WRONG: checking before when{} block — CAPTURE_DEAD hijacks state before ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA fires
            if (!ScreenCaptureService.isRunning &&
                captureWasEverRunning &&
                sheetState != SheetState.CAPTURE_DEAD) {
                sheetState = SheetState.CAPTURE_DEAD
                if (isViewAdded) updateWidget() else { showWidget(); updateWidget() }
                Log.w(TAG, "ScreenCaptureService is dead — showing CAPTURE_DEAD state")
            } else if (intent?.hasExtra(EXTRA_NET_VALUE) == true) {
                captureWasEverRunning = true
                loadExtras(intent)
                sheetState = SheetState.PILL
                if (isViewAdded) {
                    updateWidget()
                    Log.d(TAG, "Reusing existing pill for new offer")
                } else {
                    showWidget()
                    updateWidget()
                }
            }
        }
    }
    return START_STICKY
}
Hand this to DeepSeek with the instruction to apply exactly this change to OfferOverlayService.kt — the onStartCommand method only, no other changes. Then build, install, and retest the broadcast:

bash
adb -s 10.0.0.110:43013 shell am broadcast -a com.augusteenterprise.giglens.SHOW_CAMERA --es offer_id "test-offer-123" -p com.augusteenterprise.giglens
Pill should flip to the 📷 camera state immediately.

provide the python enddoc  to update

2:43 AM
Here's the Python replace script:

python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# fix_overlay_capture_dead.py
# Moves CAPTURE_DEAD check inside else branch so ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA is never hijacked

import re

FILE = "/home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/service/OfferOverlayService.kt"

OLD = '''    override fun onStartCommand(intent: Intent?, flags: Int, startId: Int): Int {
        // Check if ScreenCaptureService died mid-shift — show CAPTURE_DEAD state
        // CORRECT: only show warning if capture was previously running — not on first launch
        // WRONG: showing CAPTURE_DEAD on startup before driver enables capture
        if (!ScreenCaptureService.isRunning &&
            captureWasEverRunning &&
            sheetState != SheetState.CAPTURE_DEAD) {
            sheetState = SheetState.CAPTURE_DEAD
            if (isViewAdded) updateWidget() else { showWidget(); updateWidget() }
            Log.w(TAG, "ScreenCaptureService is dead — showing CAPTURE_DEAD state")
        }

        when (intent?.action) {
            ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA -> {
                // New offer detected — cancel any pending revert, morph to camera
                captureWasEverRunning = true
                cancelRevertTimer()
                sheetState = SheetState.CAMERA
                if (isViewAdded) updateWidget() else { showWidget(); updateWidget() }
                Log.d(TAG, "Widget morphed to CAMERA state")
                // CORRECT: stopSelf(startId) releases this transient start — service stays running via onCreate()
                // WRONG: omitting stopSelf — leaves a DEAD ConnectionRecord for every SHOW_CAMERA fired
                stopSelf(startId)
            }
            ACTION_HIDE_CAMERA -> {
                // Offer screen gone — morph back to pill or idle
                if (sheetState == SheetState.CAMERA || sheetState == SheetState.PROCESSING) {
                    sheetState = if (verdict == "UNKNOWN") SheetState.IDLE else SheetState.PILL
                    if (isViewAdded) updateWidget() else { showWidget(); updateWidget() }
                    Log.d(TAG, "Widget morphed back from CAMERA to $sheetState")
                }
                // CORRECT: stopSelf(startId) releases this transient start
                // WRONG: omitting stopSelf — leaks record even when state change was skipped
                stopSelf(startId)
            }
            else -> {
                if (intent?.hasExtra(EXTRA_NET_VALUE) == true) {
                    captureWasEverRunning = true
                    loadExtras(intent)
                    sheetState = SheetState.PILL
                    if (isViewAdded) {
                        updateWidget()
                        Log.d(TAG, "Reusing existing pill for new offer")
                    } else {
                        showWidget()
                        updateWidget()
                    }
                    // Timer disabled — pill stays in result state for entire shift
                }
            }
        }
        return START_STICKY  // restart if killed
    }'''

NEW = '''    override fun onStartCommand(intent: Intent?, flags: Int, startId: Int): Int {
        when (intent?.action) {
            ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA -> {
                // New offer detected — cancel any pending revert, morph to camera
                // CORRECT: process ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA first — before any CAPTURE_DEAD check
                // WRONG: checking ScreenCaptureService.isRunning before this block — hijacks state to CAPTURE_DEAD
                captureWasEverRunning = true
                cancelRevertTimer()
                sheetState = SheetState.CAMERA
                if (isViewAdded) updateWidget() else { showWidget(); updateWidget() }
                Log.d(TAG, "Widget morphed to CAMERA state")
                stopSelf(startId)
            }
            ACTION_HIDE_CAMERA -> {
                // Offer screen gone — morph back to pill or idle
                if (sheetState == SheetState.CAMERA || sheetState == SheetState.PROCESSING) {
                    sheetState = if (verdict == "UNKNOWN") SheetState.IDLE else SheetState.PILL
                    if (isViewAdded) updateWidget() else { showWidget(); updateWidget() }
                    Log.d(TAG, "Widget morphed back from CAMERA to $sheetState")
                }
                stopSelf(startId)
            }
            else -> {
                // CORRECT: only check CAPTURE_DEAD when no actionable intent — not before ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA
                // WRONG: checking before when{} block — CAPTURE_DEAD hijacks state before ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA fires
                if (!ScreenCaptureService.isRunning &&
                    captureWasEverRunning &&
                    sheetState != SheetState.CAPTURE_DEAD) {
                    sheetState = SheetState.CAPTURE_DEAD
                    if (isViewAdded) updateWidget() else { showWidget(); updateWidget() }
                    Log.w(TAG, "ScreenCaptureService is dead — showing CAPTURE_DEAD state")
                } else if (intent?.hasExtra(EXTRA_NET_VALUE) == true) {
                    captureWasEverRunning = true
                    loadExtras(intent)
                    sheetState = SheetState.PILL
                    if (isViewAdded) {
                        updateWidget()
                        Log.d(TAG, "Reusing existing pill for new offer")
                    } else {
                        showWidget()
                        updateWidget()
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return START_STICKY  // restart if killed
    }'''

with open(FILE, 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

if OLD not in content:
    print("❌ Anchor not found — file may have changed. Aborting.")
    exit(1)

updated = content.replace(OLD, NEW, 1)

with open(FILE, 'w') as f:
    f.write(updated)

print("✅ onStartCommand patched — CAPTURE_DEAD check moved inside else branch")
Run it:

bash
cd /home/poppa/giglens
python3 fix_overlay_capture_dead.py
Verify the anchor landed:

bash
grep -n "CAPTURE_DEAD check moved" app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/service/OfferOverlayService.kt
Then build and test the broadcast.

us python << EOF to update

2:44 AM
bash
python3 << 'EOF'
# fix_overlay_capture_dead.py
# Moves CAPTURE_DEAD check inside else branch so ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA is never hijacked

FILE = "/home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/service/OfferOverlayService.kt"

OLD = '''    override fun onStartCommand(intent: Intent?, flags: Int, startId: Int): Int {
        // Check if ScreenCaptureService died mid-shift — show CAPTURE_DEAD state
        // CORRECT: only show warning if capture was previously running — not on first launch
        // WRONG: showing CAPTURE_DEAD on startup before driver enables capture
        if (!ScreenCaptureService.isRunning &&
            captureWasEverRunning &&
            sheetState != SheetState.CAPTURE_DEAD) {
            sheetState = SheetState.CAPTURE_DEAD
            if (isViewAdded) updateWidget() else { showWidget(); updateWidget() }
            Log.w(TAG, "ScreenCaptureService is dead — showing CAPTURE_DEAD state")
        }

        when (intent?.action) {
            ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA -> {
                // New offer detected — cancel any pending revert, morph to camera
                captureWasEverRunning = true
                cancelRevertTimer()
                sheetState = SheetState.CAMERA
                if (isViewAdded) updateWidget() else { showWidget(); updateWidget() }
                Log.d(TAG, "Widget morphed to CAMERA state")
                // CORRECT: stopSelf(startId) releases this transient start — service stays running via onCreate()
                // WRONG: omitting stopSelf — leaves a DEAD ConnectionRecord for every SHOW_CAMERA fired
                stopSelf(startId)
            }
            ACTION_HIDE_CAMERA -> {
                // Offer screen gone — morph back to pill or idle
                if (sheetState == SheetState.CAMERA || sheetState == SheetState.PROCESSING) {
                    sheetState = if (verdict == "UNKNOWN") SheetState.IDLE else SheetState.PILL
                    if (isViewAdded) updateWidget() else { showWidget(); updateWidget() }
                    Log.d(TAG, "Widget morphed back from CAMERA to $sheetState")
                }
                // CORRECT: stopSelf(startId) releases this transient start
                // WRONG: omitting stopSelf — leaks record even when state change was skipped
                stopSelf(startId)
            }
            else -> {
                if (intent?.hasExtra(EXTRA_NET_VALUE) == true) {
                    captureWasEverRunning = true
                    loadExtras(intent)
                    sheetState = SheetState.PILL
                    if (isViewAdded) {
                        updateWidget()
                        Log.d(TAG, "Reusing existing pill for new offer")
                    } else {
                        showWidget()
                        updateWidget()
                    }
                    // Timer disabled — pill stays in result state for entire shift
                }
            }
        }
        return START_STICKY  // restart if killed
    }'''

NEW = '''    override fun onStartCommand(intent: Intent?, flags: Int, startId: Int): Int {
        when (intent?.action) {
            ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA -> {
                // New offer detected — cancel any pending revert, morph to camera
                // CORRECT: process ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA first — before any CAPTURE_DEAD check
                // WRONG: checking ScreenCaptureService.isRunning before this block — hijacks state to CAPTURE_DEAD
                captureWasEverRunning = true
                cancelRevertTimer()
                sheetState = SheetState.CAMERA
                if (isViewAdded) updateWidget() else { showWidget(); updateWidget() }
                Log.d(TAG, "Widget morphed to CAMERA state")
                stopSelf(startId)
            }
            ACTION_HIDE_CAMERA -> {
                // Offer screen gone — morph back to pill or idle
                if (sheetState == SheetState.CAMERA || sheetState == SheetState.PROCESSING) {
                    sheetState = if (verdict == "UNKNOWN") SheetState.IDLE else SheetState.PILL
                    if (isViewAdded) updateWidget() else { showWidget(); updateWidget() }
                    Log.d(TAG, "Widget morphed back from CAMERA to $sheetState")
                }
                stopSelf(startId)
            }
            else -> {
                // CORRECT: only check CAPTURE_DEAD when no actionable intent — not before ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA
                // WRONG: checking before when{} block — CAPTURE_DEAD hijacks state before ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA fires
                if (!ScreenCaptureService.isRunning &&
                    captureWasEverRunning &&
                    sheetState != SheetState.CAPTURE_DEAD) {
                    sheetState = SheetState.CAPTURE_DEAD
                    if (isViewAdded) updateWidget() else { showWidget(); updateWidget() }
                    Log.w(TAG, "ScreenCaptureService is dead — showing CAPTURE_DEAD state")
                } else if (intent?.hasExtra(EXTRA_NET_VALUE) == true) {
                    captureWasEverRunning = true
                    loadExtras(intent)
                    sheetState = SheetState.PILL
                    if (isViewAdded) {
                        updateWidget()
                        Log.d(TAG, "Reusing existing pill for new offer")
                    } else {
                        showWidget()
                        updateWidget()
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return START_STICKY  // restart if killed
    }'''

with open(FILE, 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

if OLD not in content:
    print("❌ Anchor not found — file may have changed. Aborting.")
    exit(1)

updated = content.replace(OLD, NEW, 1)

with open(FILE, 'w') as f:
    f.write(updated)

print("✅ onStartCommand patched — CAPTURE_DEAD check moved inside else branch")
EOF

2:47 AM
Critical finding. result=0 means the broadcast was not received by anyone — OfferOverlayService never got it. The logs show only OfferDetector activity, nothing from OfferOverlay.

The problem is ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA is defined as a broadcast action in the constants, but OfferOverlayService receives it via startService(), not sendBroadcast(). Our test broadcast is going nowhere.

Check how OfferDetectorService actually sends the SHOW_CAMERA signal:

bash
grep -n "SHOW_CAMERA\|startService\|sendBroadcast" /home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/service/OfferDetectorService.kt | head -20
Paste the output — this will show us the correct way to trigger it for testing.

91:            startService(deadIntent)
117:        // CORRECT: send SHOW_CAMERA only when offer screen detected — not on every window event
118:        // WRONG: sending SHOW_CAMERA on every TYPE_WINDOW_CONTENT_CHANGED — camera blinks on
139:                // CORRECT: send SHOW_CAMERA only on confirmed new offer screen
140:                // WRONG: sending SHOW_CAMERA on every window event — camera blinks randomly

144:                    Log.d(TAG, "Offer screen confirmed — sending SHOW_CAMERA")

2:49 AM
The service is not exported and can't be started externally via adb after Android 12. That's actually correct security behavior — but it means our test method is wrong. The service can only be started from within the app process.

Let's verify the fix is actually working by triggering it the real way — through OfferDetectorService which calls startService() internally. We need to simulate an offer screen appearing via the accessibility service.

First, confirm the app is actually running with capture active:

bash
adb -s 10.0.0.110:43013 shell dumpsys activity services com.augusteenterprise.giglens | grep -E "ServiceRecord|Running|OfferOverlay|OfferDetector|ScreenCapture"
Then let's check if OfferDetectorService is actually calling startService(SHOW_CAMERA) when isOffer=true fires — look at the full trigger block:

bash

2:51 AM
The startService(SHOW_CAMERA) call is wired correctly. So the signal is being sent — but the pill isn't changing.

The real question now is whether onStartCommand is actually being reached. Let's add a log probe by checking what logcat shows during a real offer detection cycle. Clear logcat, trigger a real offer on DoorDash, then immediately pull logs:

bash
adb -s 10.0.0.110:43013 logcat -c && echo "Logcat cleared — go trigger a DoorDash offer now"
Then after an offer appears on screen:

bash
adb -s 10.0.0.110:43013 logcat -d --pid=$(adb -s 10.0.0.110:43013 shell pidof com.augusteenterprise.giglens) | grep -iE "OfferOverlay|morphed|CAMERA|state|onStart|widget|dead|confirmed" | head -30
However — before going on the road, let me look at one more thing. The startForegroundCount:0 on both OfferOverlayService and ScreenCaptureService is suspicious. Check if ScreenCaptureService.isRunning is actually true right now:

bash
adb -s 10.0.0.110:43013 logcat -d --pid=$(adb -s 10.0.0.110:43013 shell pidof com.augusteenterprise.giglens) | grep -iE "ScreenCapture|isRunning|captureRunning" | tail -20
If ScreenCaptureService.isRunning is returning false even though the service is running, the CAPTURE_DEAD branch will still fire inside the else block and block the EXTRA_NET_VALUE path — but more importantly, captureWasEverRunning would never be set to true from the ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA branch if it's never reached. Paste the output.

(base) poppa@milo-dev:~/giglens$ adb -s 10.0.0.110:43013 logcat -d --pid=$(adb -s 10.0.0.110:43013 shell pidof com.augusteenterprise.giglens) | grep -iE "ScreenCapture|isRunning|captureRunning" | tail -20

127-                captureWasEverRunning = true

132-                stopSelf(startId)
133-            }
134-            ACTION_HIDE_CAMERA -> {
135-                // Offer screen gone — morph back to pill or idle
136-                if (sheetState == SheetState.CAMERA || sheetState == SheetState.PROCESSING) {
137-                    sheetState = if (verdict == "UNKNOWN") SheetState.IDLE else SheetState.PILL
138-                    if (isViewAdded) updateWidget() else { showWidget(); updateWidget() }
139-                    Log.d(TAG, "Widget morphed back from CAMERA to $sheetState")
140-                }
141-                stopSelf(startId)
142-            }
143-            else -> {
144-                // CORRECT: only check CAPTURE_DEAD when no actionable intent — not before ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA
145-                // WRONG: checking before when{} block — CAPTURE_DEAD hijacks state before ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA fires
146-                if (!ScreenCaptureService.isRunning &&
147-                    captureWasEverRunning &&
148-                    sheetState != SheetState.CAPTURE_DEAD) {
149-                    sheetState = SheetState.CAPTURE_DEAD
150-                    if (isViewAdded) updateWidget() else { showWidget(); updateWidget() }
151-                    Log.w(TAG, "ScreenCaptureService is dead — showing CAPTURE_DEAD state")
152-                } else if (intent?.hasExtra(EXTRA_NET_VALUE) == true) {
153-                    captureWasEverRunning = true
154-                    loadExtras(intent)
155-                    sheetState = SheetState.PILL
156-                    if (isViewAdded) {
157-                        updateWidget()
158-                        Log.d(TAG, "Reusing existing pill for new offer")
159-                    } else {
160-                        showWidget()
161-                        updateWidget()
162-                    }
163-                }
164-            }
165-        }
166-        return START_STICKY  // restart if killed
167-    }
168-
169-    // ── Configurable auto-revert timer with countdown tick ────────────────────
170-    private fun startRevertTimer() {
171-        cancelRevertTimer()
172-        secondsRemaining = revertDelaySeconds()
173-        Log.d(TAG, "Revert timer started (${secondsRemaining}s)")
174-
175-        // Tick every second to update the countdown on the pill
176-        // CORRECT: pill stays in result state for entire shift — no auto-revert to IDLE
177-        // WRONG: reverting to IDLE after 60s — driver loses last offer verdict mid-shift
178-        // Timer cancelled immediately — countdown display removed from pill label
179-        Log.d(TAG, "Revert timer disabled — pill persists for shift duration")
180-    }

2:52 AM

Single process, single PID. So both services are in the same process — startService() should work.

2:56 AM
Same export block. Let's stop chasing the log angle — the service IS running (lastStartId=21 proves onStartCommand is being called). The logging suppression is a red herring.

The real question is why the pill isn't visually changing. Let's look at updateWidget() more carefully. The issue may be that windowManager.updateViewLayout() is being called on a non-UI thread, or rootView is being rebuilt but the WindowManager isn't reflecting it.

Check what thread OfferDetectorService calls startService from:

bash
grep -n -B5 -A5 "startService.*SHOW_CAMERA\|ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA" /home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/service/OfferDetectorService.kt | head -40
And check if there's a Handler or MainLooper wrapping the startService call, or if it's being called directly from the accessibility thread:

bash
grep -n "onAccessibilityEvent\|coroutineScope\|launch\|withContext\|Dispatchers" /home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/service/OfferDetectorService.kt | head -20
Paste both — if startService is being called from the accessibility thread and updateWidget touches the WindowManager from a background thread, Android will silently drop the view update on Android 16.

One thing to note. The pill was changing BEFORE the change to the mainui

prefs.edit().putBoolean("floating_button_enabled", isChecked).apply() if (isChecked && !ScreenCaptureService.isRunning) { showCaptureOnboardingFlow() } else if (!isChecked) { if (ScreenCaptureService.isRunning) stop

pasted

2:58 AM
Found it. Look at this block in onResume:

kotlin
if (android.os.Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES.S) {
    startService(overlayIntent)  // ← Android 12+ uses startService, NOT startForegroundService
} else if ...
On Android 16 (SDK 36), Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= S (31) is true — so it calls startService() instead of startForegroundService(). On Android 16, a service that needs to be foreground must be started with startForegroundService(). Using plain startService() means the service starts but gets killed almost immediately by the OS before onStartCommand can do anything useful.

The fix is simple — always use startForegroundService() on Android 8+ (O=26), which covers Android 16:

bash
sed -n '107,120p' /home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/ui/MainActivity.kt
Then apply this fix:

bash
python3 << 'EOF'
FILE = "/home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/ui/MainActivity.kt"

OLD = '''            val overlayIntent = Intent(this, OfferOverlayService::class.java)
            if (android.os.Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES.S) {
                startService(overlayIntent)
            } else if (android.os.Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
                startForegroundService(overlayIntent)
            } else {
                startService(overlayIntent)
            }'''

NEW = '''            val overlayIntent = Intent(this, OfferOverlayService::class.java)
            // CORRECT: always startForegroundService on Android O+ — service calls startForeground() in onCreate()
            // WRONG: startService on Android 12+ — OS kills it before onStartCommand fires on Android 16
            if (android.os.Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
                startForegroundService(overlayIntent)
            } else {
                startService(overlayIntent)
            }'''

with open(FILE, 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

if OLD not in content:
    print("❌ Anchor not found — aborting.")
    exit(1)

with open(FILE, 'w') as f:
    f.write(content.replace(OLD, NEW, 1))

print("✅ startForegroundService fix applied to onResume")
EOF
Then build and install:

bash
cd /home/poppa/giglens && ./gradlew assembleDebug 2>&1 | tail -5
private fun startCaptureService(resultCode: Int, data: Intent) {
        pendingScreenCaptureGrant = false
        val serviceIntent = Intent(this, ScreenCaptureService::class.java).apply {
            putExtra(ScreenCaptureService.EXTRA_RESULT_CODE, resultCode)
            putExtra(ScreenCaptureService.EXTRA_RESULT_DATA, data)
        }
        if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
            startForegroundService(serviceIntent)
        } else {
            startService(serviceIntent)
        }
        // CORRECT: sync DB so SettingsActivity reflects enabled state
        // WRONG:   only updating SharedPreferences — Settings shows wrong state
        lifecycleScope.launch {

Show more

Two things confirmed:

✅ OfferOverlayService is now logging — Widget added to WindowManager — the startForegroundService fix worked
🔴 New problem — ScreenCaptureService is dead immediately on launch, before you even start capture
OfferDetector is spamming CAPTURE_DEAD signals because ScreenCaptureService.isRunning is false. This is racing — the overlay starts, detector sees capture is dead, floods CAPTURE_DEAD intents before you can even tap the capture button.

You need to start MediaProjection capture now to stop the flood. Tap the capture button in the app and accept the prompt.

3:04 AM
Zero output — confirmed and SHOW_CAMERA never logged. The detector sees isOffer=true but never reaches the "Offer screen confirmed" block. That means the fingerprint/cooldown gate is blocking it.

Show me the full block around the fingerprint check:

bash
sed -n '125,165p' /home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/service/OfferDetectorService.kt
// Walk the accessibility tree looking for offer keywords
        val rootNode = rootInActiveWindow ?: return
        // CORRECT: try/finally guarantees recycle() even if looksLikeOfferScreen() throws
        // WRONG: relying on manual recycle() calls — any exception mid-walk leaks native node object
        try {
            if (looksLikeOfferScreen(rootNode)) {
                val fingerprint = buildOfferFingerprint(rootNode)
                if (fingerprint == lastOfferFingerprint) {
                    // Same offer still on screen — don't re-trigger
                    return
                }
                Log.i(TAG, "NEW offer screen detected — signaling capture")
                lastOfferFingerprint = fingerprint
                lastCaptureTime = now
                // CORRECT: send SHOW_CAMERA only on confirmed new offer screen
                // WRONG: sending SHOW_CAMERA on every window event — camera blinks randomly
                val now2 = System.currentTimeMillis()
                if (now2 - lastShowCameraMs >= SHOW_CAMERA_COOLDOWN_MS) {
                    lastShowCameraMs = now2
                    Log.d(TAG, "Offer screen confirmed — sending SHOW_CAMERA")
                    startService(Intent(applicationContext, OfferOverlayService::class.java).apply {
                        action = ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA
                    })
                }
                signalCapture()
            } else {
                // No offer screen detected — hide camera button if it was showing
                // CORRECT: send HIDE_CAMERA when offer screen gone — camera button clears
                // WRONG: leaving camera button visible after offer dismissed — confuses driver
                if (lastOfferFingerprint.isNotEmpty()) {
                    Log.d(TAG, "Offer screen gone — sending HIDE_CAMERA")
                    startService(Intent(applicationContext, OfferOverlayService::class.java).apply {
                        action = ACTION_HIDE_CAMERA
                    })
                    lastOfferFingerprint = ""
                }
            }
        } finally {
            rootNode.recycle()
        }
    }

Show more
3:05 AM
Found it. This line is the culprit:

kotlin
if (fingerprint == lastOfferFingerprint) {
    // Same offer still on screen — don't re-trigger
    return
}
The fingerprint matches on the very first event, which means lastOfferFingerprint is being set to the same value before SHOW_CAMERA ever fires. This happens because signalCapture() at the bottom triggers another accessibility event, which re-enters onAccessibilityEvent, builds the same fingerprint, matches, and returns — skipping SHOW_CAMERA every subsequent time.

But the deeper issue is the first hit — SHOW_CAMERA should fire on the first new fingerprint. Check what lastOfferFingerprint is initialized to and whether it's being reset correctly:

bash
grep -n "lastOfferFingerprint\|lastShowCameraMs\|lastCaptureTime" /home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/service/OfferDetectorService.kt | head -20
And check buildOfferFingerprint:

bash
grep -n -A 10 "fun buildOfferFingerprint" /home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/service/OfferDetectorService.kt
Paste both — I want to see if the fingerprint is returning an empty string or a value that accidentally matches the initial state.

27:    private var lastShowCameraMs = 0L
44:    private var lastCaptureTime = 0L
45:    private var lastOfferFingerprint = ""  // detects new vs same offer screen
123:        if (now - lastCaptureTime < CAPTURE_COOLDOWN_MS) return
132:                if (fingerprint == lastOfferFingerprint) {
137:                lastOfferFingerprint = fingerprint
138:                lastCaptureTime = now
142:                if (now2 - lastShowCameraMs >= SHOW_CAMERA_COOLDOWN_MS) {
143:                    lastShowCameraMs = now2
154:                if (lastOfferFingerprint.isNotEmpty()) {
159:                    lastOfferFingerprint = ""

180-                sb.append(text).append("|")
181-            }

3:06 AM
Found it. Look at line 123:

kotlin
if (now - lastCaptureTime < CAPTURE_COOLDOWN_MS) return
CAPTURE_COOLDOWN_MS = 3000L and lastCaptureTime is set at line 138 — before SHOW_CAMERA fires at line 142. So the sequence is:

First event: fingerprint is new → sets lastCaptureTime = now → sends SHOW_CAMERA ✅
Second event (100ms later): now - lastCaptureTime < 3000 → returns immediately at line 123 — never reaches fingerprint check
This repeats for 3 full seconds
After 3s: new event comes in, fingerprint matches lastOfferFingerprint → returns at line 132
So SHOW_CAMERA fires exactly once on the very first event, but lastShowCameraMs check at line 142 may block even that. Check the logcat for "NEW offer screen detected":

bash
adb -s 10.0.0.110:43013 logcat -d --pid=$(adb -s 10.0.0.110:43013 shell pidof com.augusteenterprise.giglens) | grep -iE "NEW offer|signaling capture" | tail -10
If "NEW offer screen detected" never appears, the CAPTURE_COOLDOWN is eating every single event before the fingerprint check. The fix is to move the cooldown check to after the fingerprint check and SHOW_CAMERA send:

bash
grep -n "CAPTURE_COOLDOWN_MS\|lastCaptureTime" /home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/service/OfferDetectorService.kt | head -10
Paste the logcat output first so we confirm which gate is actually blocking.

(base) poppa@milo-dev:~/giglens$ adb -s 10.0.0.110:43013 logcat -d --pid=$(adb -s 10.0.0.110:43013 shell pidof com.augusteenterprise.giglens) | grep -iE "NEW offer|signaling capture" | tail -10
(base) poppa@milo-dev:~/giglens$ grep -n "CAPTURE_COOLDOWN_MS\|lastCaptureTime" /home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/service/OfferDetectorService.kt | head -10
35:        private const val CAPTURE_COOLDOWN_MS = 3000L
44:    private var lastCaptureTime = 0L
123:        if (now - lastCaptureTime < CAPTURE_COOLDOWN_MS) return
138:                lastCaptureTime = now

3:07 AM
Confirmed. "NEW offer screen detected" never appears — CAPTURE_COOLDOWN_MS at line 123 is eating every event before the fingerprint check is ever reached.

The fix: move the cooldown gate to after SHOW_CAMERA fires, so it only throttles signalCapture(), not the camera trigger. Show me the full block first:

bash
sed -n '118,150p' /home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/service/OfferDetectorService.kt
// WRONG: sending SHOW_CAMERA on every TYPE_WINDOW_CONTENT_CHANGED — camera blinks on
        //        map redraws, navigation updates, earnings screen, any DoorDash UI change
  // Cooldown check
        val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
        if (now - lastCaptureTime < CAPTURE_COOLDOWN_MS) return
        // Walk the accessibility tree looking for offer keywords
        val rootNode = rootInActiveWindow ?: return
        // CORRECT: try/finally guarantees recycle() even if looksLikeOfferScreen() throws
        // WRONG: relying on manual recycle() calls — any exception mid-walk leaks native node object
        try {
            if (looksLikeOfferScreen(rootNode)) {
                val fingerprint = buildOfferFingerprint(rootNode)
                if (fingerprint == lastOfferFingerprint) {
                    // Same offer still on screen — don't re-trigger
                    return
                }
                Log.i(TAG, "NEW offer screen detected — signaling capture")
                lastOfferFingerprint = fingerprint
                lastCaptureTime = now
                // CORRECT: send SHOW_CAMERA only on confirmed new offer screen
                // WRONG: sending SHOW_CAMERA on every window event — camera blinks randomly
                val now2 = System.currentTimeMillis()
                if (now2 - lastShowCameraMs >= SHOW_CAMERA_COOLDOWN_MS) {
                    lastShowCameraMs = now2
                    Log.d(TAG, "Offer screen confirmed — sending SHOW_CAMERA")
                    startService(Intent(applicationContext, OfferOverlayService::class.java).apply {
                        action = ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA
                    })
                }
                signalCapture()
            } else {

Show more
3:07 AM
Clear picture now. The fix is to move the cooldown check to only gate signalCapture(), not the entire block. SHOW_CAMERA should only be gated by the fingerprint check:

bash
python3 << 'EOF'
FILE = "/home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/service/OfferDetectorService.kt"

OLD = '''  // Cooldown check
        val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
        if (now - lastCaptureTime < CAPTURE_COOLDOWN_MS) return
        // Walk the accessibility tree looking for offer keywords
        val rootNode = rootInActiveWindow ?: return
        // CORRECT: try/finally guarantees recycle() even if looksLikeOfferScreen() throws
        // WRONG: relying on manual recycle() calls — any exception mid-walk leaks native node object
        try {
            if (looksLikeOfferScreen(rootNode)) {
                val fingerprint = buildOfferFingerprint(rootNode)
                if (fingerprint == lastOfferFingerprint) {
                    // Same offer still on screen — don't re-trigger
                    return
                }
                Log.i(TAG, "NEW offer screen detected — signaling capture")
                lastOfferFingerprint = fingerprint
                lastCaptureTime = now
                // CORRECT: send SHOW_CAMERA only on confirmed new offer screen
                // WRONG: sending SHOW_CAMERA on every window event — camera blinks randomly
                val now2 = System.currentTimeMillis()
                if (now2 - lastShowCameraMs >= SHOW_CAMERA_COOLDOWN_MS) {
                    lastShowCameraMs = now2
                    Log.d(TAG, "Offer screen confirmed — sending SHOW_CAMERA")
                    startService(Intent(applicationContext, OfferOverlayService::class.java).apply {
                        action = ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA
                    })
                }
                signalCapture()
            } else {'''

NEW = '''  // CORRECT: only apply cooldown to signalCapture() — not to SHOW_CAMERA or fingerprint check
        // WRONG: early return on cooldown before fingerprint check — SHOW_CAMERA never fires on real offers
        val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
        // Walk the accessibility tree looking for offer keywords
        val rootNode = rootInActiveWindow ?: return
        // CORRECT: try/finally guarantees recycle() even if looksLikeOfferScreen() throws
        // WRONG: relying on manual recycle() calls — any exception mid-walk leaks native node object
        try {
            if (looksLikeOfferScreen(rootNode)) {
                val fingerprint = buildOfferFingerprint(rootNode)
                if (fingerprint == lastOfferFingerprint) {
                    // Same offer still on screen — don't re-trigger
                    return
                }
                Log.i(TAG, "NEW offer screen detected — signaling capture")
                lastOfferFingerprint = fingerprint
                // CORRECT: send SHOW_CAMERA on every new fingerprint — gated only by cooldown below
                // WRONG: gating SHOW_CAMERA behind CAPTURE_COOLDOWN_MS — camera never fires on real offers
                val now2 = System.currentTimeMillis()
                if (now2 - lastShowCameraMs >= SHOW_CAMERA_COOLDOWN_MS) {
                    lastShowCameraMs = now2
                    Log.d(TAG, "Offer screen confirmed — sending SHOW_CAMERA")
                    startService(Intent(applicationContext, OfferOverlayService::class.java).apply {
                        action = ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA
                    })
                }
                // Cooldown gates signalCapture() only — not SHOW_CAMERA
                if (now - lastCaptureTime < CAPTURE_COOLDOWN_MS) return
                lastCaptureTime = now
                signalCapture()
            } else {'''

with open(FILE, 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

if OLD not in content:
    print("❌ Anchor not found — aborting.")
    exit(1)

with open(FILE, 'w') as f:
    f.write(content.replace(OLD, NEW, 1))

print("✅ CAPTURE_COOLDOWN moved after SHOW_CAMERA — camera will now fire on real offers")
EOF
Then build and install:

bash
cd /home/poppa/giglens && ./gradlew assembleDebug 2>&1 | tail -5
❌ Anchor not found — aborting.

        // WRONG: relying on manual recycle() calls M-bM-^@M-^T any exception mid-walk leaks native node object$
        try {$
            if (looksLikeOfferScreen(rootNode)) {$
                val fingerprint = buildOfferFingerprint(rootNode)$

                    // Same offer still on screen M-bM-^@M-^T don't re-trigger$
                    return$
                }$
                Log.i(TAG, "NEW offer screen detected M-bM-^@M-^T signaling capture")$
                lastOfferFingerprint = fingerprint$
                lastCaptureTime = now$
                // CORRECT: send SHOW_CAMERA only on confirmed new offer screen$
                // WRONG: sending SHOW_CAMERA on every window event M-bM-^@M-^T camera blinks randomly$
                val now2 = System.currentTimeMillis()$
                if (now2 - lastShowCameraMs >= SHOW_CAMERA_COOLDOWN_MS) {$
                    lastShowCameraMs = now2$
                    Log.d(TAG, "Offer screen confirmed M-bM-^@M-^T sending SHOW_CAMERA")$
                    startService(Intent(applicationContext, OfferOverlayService::class.java).apply {$
                        action = ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA$
                    })$
                }$
                signalCapture()$
            } else {$

Show more
3:08 AM
The em dashes (M-bM-^@M-^T) are the issue — they're UTF-8 em dashes that don't match the ASCII dashes in the Python string. Use a line-number based replacement instead:

bash
python3 << 'EOF'
FILE = "/home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/service/OfferDetectorService.kt"

with open(FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    lines = f.readlines()

# Find the cooldown check line
cooldown_line = None
for i, line in enumerate(lines):
    if 'if (now - lastCaptureTime < CAPTURE_COOLDOWN_MS) return' in line:
        cooldown_line = i
        break

if cooldown_line is None:
    print("❌ Cooldown line not found — aborting.")
    exit(1)

print(f"Found cooldown at line {cooldown_line + 1}: {lines[cooldown_line].rstrip()}")

# Find lastCaptureTime = now line (after fingerprint set)
capture_time_line = None
for i in range(cooldown_line, cooldown_line + 30):
    if 'lastCaptureTime = now' in lines[i]:
        capture_time_line = i
        break

if capture_time_line is None:
    print("❌ lastCaptureTime = now not found — aborting.")
    exit(1)

print(f"Found lastCaptureTime at line {capture_time_line + 1}: {lines[capture_time_line].rstrip()}")

# Find signalCapture() line
signal_line = None
for i in range(capture_time_line, capture_time_line + 20):
    if 'signalCapture()' in lines[i]:
        signal_line = i
        break

if signal_line is None:
    print("❌ signalCapture() not found — aborting.")
    exit(1)

print(f"Found signalCapture at line {signal_line + 1}: {lines[signal_line].rstrip()}")

# Step 1: Remove the early cooldown return (lines cooldown_line-1 to cooldown_line+1)
# Replace cooldown check with just `val now = ...` (keep that line, remove the if return)
# The line before is blank, line is the if return, line after is blank
lines[cooldown_line] = '        // CORRECT: cooldown gates signalCapture() only \u2014 not SHOW_CAMERA or fingerprint check\n        // WRONG: early return on cooldown before fingerprint check \u2014 SHOW_CAMERA never fires on real offers\n'

# Step 2: Remove lastCaptureTime = now from its current position (after fingerprint set)
lines[capture_time_line] = ''

# Step 3: Before signalCapture(), insert cooldown gate + lastCaptureTime = now
indent = '                '
lines[signal_line] = (
    f'{indent}// Cooldown gates signalCapture() only \u2014 not SHOW_CAMERA\n'
    f'{indent}if (now - lastCaptureTime < CAPTURE_COOLDOWN_MS) return\n'
    f'{indent}lastCaptureTime = now\n'
    f'{indent}signalCapture()\n'
)

with open(FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    f.writelines(lines)

print("✅ CAPTURE_COOLDOWN moved after SHOW_CAMERA — camera will now fire on real offers")
EOF
Then verify:

bash
sed -n '118,155p' /home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/service/OfferDetectorService.kt
// WRONG: sending SHOW_CAMERA on every TYPE_WINDOW_CONTENT_CHANGED — camera blinks on
        //        map redraws, navigation updates, earnings screen, any DoorDash UI change
  // Cooldown check
        val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
        // CORRECT: cooldown gates signalCapture() only — not SHOW_CAMERA or fingerprint check
        // WRONG: early return on cooldown before fingerprint check — SHOW_CAMERA never fires on real offers
        // Walk the accessibility tree looking for offer keywords
        val rootNode = rootInActiveWindow ?: return
        // CORRECT: try/finally guarantees recycle() even if looksLikeOfferScreen() throws
        // WRONG: relying on manual recycle() calls — any exception mid-walk leaks native node object
        try {
            if (looksLikeOfferScreen(rootNode)) {
                val fingerprint = buildOfferFingerprint(rootNode)
                if (fingerprint == lastOfferFingerprint) {
                    // Same offer still on screen — don't re-trigger
                    return
                }
                Log.i(TAG, "NEW offer screen detected — signaling capture")
                lastOfferFingerprint = fingerprint
                // CORRECT: send SHOW_CAMERA only on confirmed new offer screen
                // WRONG: sending SHOW_CAMERA on every window event — camera blinks randomly
                val now2 = System.currentTimeMillis()
                if (now2 - lastShowCameraMs >= SHOW_CAMERA_COOLDOWN_MS) {
                    lastShowCameraMs = now2
                    Log.d(TAG, "Offer screen confirmed — sending SHOW_CAMERA")
                    startService(Intent(applicationContext, OfferOverlayService::class.java).apply {
                        action = ACTION_SHOW_CAMERA
                    })
                }
                // Cooldown gates signalCapture() only — not SHOW_CAMERA
                if (now - lastCaptureTime < CAPTURE_COOLDOWN_MS) return
                lastCaptureTime = now
                signalCapture()
            } else {
                // No offer screen detected — hide camera button if it was showing
                // CORRECT: send HIDE_CAMERA when offer screen gone — camera button clears

3:13 AM
The GigLens overlay window is completely absent from the window list. Only DoorDash and MainActivity are showing — the TYPE_APPLICATION_OVERLAY window for GigLens is not registered with WindowManager at all.

This means OfferOverlayService is running but its overlay view was never added, or was removed. Notice mBaseLayer=21000 for both DoorDash and GigLens — that's the app layer, not the overlay layer (which should be 111000).

Check if the overlay was added and then removed:

bash
adb -s 10.0.0.110:43013 logcat -d --pid=$(adb -s 10.0.0.110:43013 shell pidof com.augusteenterprise.giglens) | grep -iE "Widget added|Widget already|addView|removeView|isViewAdded|BadToken|overlay" | tail -20
My suspicion: showWidget() was called, isViewAdded was set to true, but then the service was killed and restarted by START_STICKY — on restart isViewAdded defaults to false but the old view is gone, so addView either throws BadTokenException or the view gets added to a dead window context.

That means OfferOverlayService.onCreate() is not being called — the service is being reused from a previous instance without going through onCreate. The START_STICKY restart is reusing the service object but showWidget() only runs in onCreate.

3:15 AM
Zero OfferOverlayService logs from PID 18583 — onCreate never fired. The service is running (dumpsys shows it) but it's the same instance from before the force-stop, kept alive by the system. onCreate only fires once per service instance.

The root issue: showWidget() ran on an earlier PID, that process died, but the WindowManager view died with it. When the new process started, OfferOverlayService was restarted by START_STICKY but onCreate was never called — so showWidget() never ran, isViewAdded is false, and the overlay window was never re-added.

The fix: force a clean restart by stopping and restarting the service from MainActivity on every onResume. Let me see the current onResume overlay start block:

bash
sed -n '195,220p' /home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/ui/MainActivity.kt
The fix will be to always stop and restart OfferOverlayService on onResume rather than checking isRunning — this guarantees onCreate always fires and showWidget() always runs.

override fun onResume() {
        super.onResume()
        // CORRECT: start overlay service while app is in foreground — avoids Android 12+ bg restriction
        // WRONG:   starting from ScreenCaptureService (background) — throws ForegroundServiceStartNotAllowedException
        // CORRECT: start overlay as soon as overlay permission is granted — pill should
        //          always be visible when toggle is ON, independent of screen capture state
        // WRONG:   gating on ScreenCaptureService.isRunning — pill never shows until DoorDash opens
        val prefs = getSharedPreferences("giglens_ui", MODE_PRIVATE)
        val toggleEnabled = prefs.getBoolean("floating_button_enabled", false)
        val canDraw = android.provider.Settings.canDrawOverlays(this)
        Log.d("MainActivity", "onResume: isRunning=${OfferOverlayService.isRunning} toggleEnabled=$toggleEnabled canDraw=$canDraw")
        if (!OfferOverlayService.isRunning && toggleEnabled && canDraw) {
            Log.d("MainActivity", "onResume: starting OfferOverlayService")
            val overlayIntent = Intent(this, OfferOverlayService::class.java)
            // CORRECT: always startForegroundService on Android O+ — service calls startForeground() in onCreate()
            // WRONG: startService on Android 12+ — OS kills it before onStartCommand fires on Android 16
            if (android.os.Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
                startForegroundService(overlayIntent)
            } else {
                startService(overlayIntent)
            }
        }
        // Handle restart_capture flag from CAPTURE_DEAD pill tap
        // CORRECT: re-request MediaProjection when driver taps the warning pill

Show more
3:16 AM
The problem is clear — if (!OfferOverlayService.isRunning) prevents restart because isRunning is still true from the old instance. Apply this fix:

bash
python3 << 'EOF'
FILE = "/home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/ui/MainActivity.kt"

OLD = '''        Log.d("MainActivity", "onResume: isRunning=${OfferOverlayService.isRunning} toggleEnabled=$toggleEnabled canDraw=$canDraw")
        if (!OfferOverlayService.isRunning && toggleEnabled && canDraw) {
            Log.d("MainActivity", "onResume: starting OfferOverlayService")
            val overlayIntent = Intent(this, OfferOverlayService::class.java)
            // CORRECT: always startForegroundService on Android O+ — service calls startForeground() in onCreate()
            // WRONG: startService on Android 12+ — OS kills it before onStartCommand fires on Android 16
            if (android.os.Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
                startForegroundService(overlayIntent)
            } else {
                startService(overlayIntent)
            }
        }'''

NEW = '''        Log.d("MainActivity", "onResume: isRunning=${OfferOverlayService.isRunning} toggleEnabled=$toggleEnabled canDraw=$canDraw")
        if (toggleEnabled && canDraw) {
            // CORRECT: always stop + restart OfferOverlayService on onResume — guarantees onCreate fires
            //          and showWidget() re-adds the overlay window after process death/restart
            // WRONG: gating on !isRunning — START_STICKY reuses old instance without calling onCreate,
            //        overlay window never re-added after process restarts
            if (OfferOverlayService.isRunning) {
                Log.d("MainActivity", "onResume: stopping existing OfferOverlayService for clean restart")
                stopService(Intent(this, OfferOverlayService::class.java))
            }
            Log.d("MainActivity", "onResume: starting OfferOverlayService")
            val overlayIntent = Intent(this, OfferOverlayService::class.java)
            if (android.os.Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
                startForegroundService(overlayIntent)
            } else {
                startService(overlayIntent)
            }
        }'''

with open(FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    content = f.read()

if OLD not in content:
    print("❌ Anchor not found — aborting.")
    exit(1)

with open(FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    f.write(content.replace(OLD, NEW, 1))

print("✅ onResume patched — OfferOverlayService always restarts clean on resume")
EOF
Then build and install:

bash
cd /home/poppa/giglens && ./gradlew assembleDebug 2>&1 | tail -5 && adb -s 10.0.0.110:43013 install -r app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk
will that effect battey usage?

3:16 AM
Good question. Yes, slightly — but it's minimal because:

onResume only fires when the driver opens MainActivity, which is rare during a shift (once at start, maybe once if they tap the CAPTURE_DEAD pill)
The stop/restart takes ~100ms and happens in the foreground
The overlay service itself stays running the entire shift — we're only restarting it when MainActivity comes to foreground, not continuously
The alternative would be to fix isViewAdded state persistence properly — detect when the window was lost and re-add it without restarting the service. That's cleaner long-term but more complex.

For now the stop/restart on onResume is the right pragmatic fix. A driver opens MainActivity maybe 2-3 times per shift max. The battery impact is negligible compared to the continuous OCR/MediaProjection capture already running.

Shall I proceed with the build?

what does that mean? A driver opens MainActivity maybe 2-3 times per shift max

3:18 AM
Good catch — let me be more precise.

onResume fires every time MainActivity comes to the foreground, which includes:

App launch at shift start
Returning from Settings
Returning from any other app
Tapping the CAPTURE_DEAD pill (which launches MainActivity)
Phone waking up if MainActivity was the last open app
So in practice it could fire 10-20+ times per shift, not 2-3. I understated that.

However — the stop/restart is still very lightweight. It's just two service lifecycle calls, no network, no DB, no OCR. The overlay pill disappears and reappears in under a second. The real battery drain is the continuous MediaProjection + accessibility scanning already running, which this doesn't touch at all.

The only real driver annoyance is the pill briefly disappearing and reappearing every time they switch back to MainActivity. That's acceptable for now but worth revisiting.

Want to proceed with this fix, or would you prefer the cleaner approach — detecting the lost window and re-adding it without restarting the service?

Is the better for the battery: detecting the lost window and re-adding

3:19 AM
Yes, definitely better. No service restart, no pill flicker, no unnecessary onCreate cycles. The overlay just silently re-adds itself if the window is gone.

The fix is in onStartCommand — add a window validity check at the top:

kotlin
if (isViewAdded) {
    // verify window is still attached — re-add if lost after process restart
    try {
        windowManager.updateViewLayout(rootView!!, layoutParams!!)
    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
        // window was lost — re-add it
        isViewAdded = false
        showWidget()
    }
}
This is lightweight — updateViewLayout is a single binder call, no redraw, no service restart. If the window is healthy it costs ~1ms. If it's lost, it silently re-adds it.

Want me to write the patch for this approach instead?

yes

3:20 AM
bash
python3 << 'EOF'
FILE = "/home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/ui/MainActivity.kt"

OLD = '''        Log.d("MainActivity", "onResume: isRunning=${OfferOverlayService.isRunning} toggleEnabled=$toggleEnabled canDraw=$canDraw")
        if (toggleEnabled && canDraw) {
            // CORRECT: always stop + restart OfferOverlayService on onResume — guarantees onCreate fires
            //          and showWidget() re-adds the overlay window after process death/restart
            // WRONG: gating on !isRunning — START_STICKY reuses old instance without calling onCreate,
            //        overlay window never re-added after process restarts
            if (OfferOverlayService.isRunning) {
                Log.d("MainActivity", "onResume: stopping existing OfferOverlayService for clean restart")
                stopService(Intent(this, OfferOverlayService::class.java))
            }
            Log.d("MainActivity", "onResume: starting OfferOverlayService")
            val overlayIntent = Intent(this, OfferOverlayService::class.java)
            if (android.os.Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
                startForegroundService(overlayIntent)
            } else {
                startService(overlayIntent)
            }
        }'''

NEW = '''        Log.d("MainActivity", "onResume: isRunning=${OfferOverlayService.isRunning} toggleEnabled=$toggleEnabled canDraw=$canDraw")
        if (!OfferOverlayService.isRunning && toggleEnabled && canDraw) {
            Log.d("MainActivity", "onResume: starting OfferOverlayService")
            val overlayIntent = Intent(this, OfferOverlayService::class.java)
            // CORRECT: always startForegroundService on Android O+ — service calls startForeground() in onCreate()
            // WRONG: startService on Android 12+ — OS kills it before onStartCommand fires on Android 16
            if (android.os.Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
                startForegroundService(overlayIntent)
            } else {
                startService(overlayIntent)
            }
        }'''

with open(FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    content = f.read()

if OLD not in content:
    print("❌ Anchor not found — aborting.")
    exit(1)

with open(FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    f.write(content.replace(OLD, NEW, 1))

print("✅ MainActivity reverted — window recovery handled in OfferOverlayService")
EOF
Now patch OfferOverlayService to detect and recover lost window:

bash
python3 << 'EOF'
FILE = "/home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/service/OfferOverlayService.kt"

OLD = '''    override fun onStartCommand(intent: Intent?, flags: Int, startId: Int): Int {
        when (intent?.action) {'''

NEW = '''    override fun onStartCommand(intent: Intent?, flags: Int, startId: Int): Int {
        // CORRECT: verify overlay window is still attached on every onStartCommand — re-add silently if lost
        // WRONG: assuming isViewAdded means window is valid — process restart loses window without clearing flag
        if (isViewAdded && rootView != null && layoutParams != null) {
            try {
                windowManager.updateViewLayout(rootView!!, layoutParams!!)
            } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
                Log.w(TAG, "Overlay window lost — re-adding silently: ${e.message}")
                isViewAdded = false
                showWidget()
            }
        } else if (!isViewAdded) {
            showWidget()
        }
        when (intent?.action) {'''

with open(FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    content = f.read()

if OLD not in content:
    print("❌ Anchor not found — aborting.")
    exit(1)

with open(FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    f.write(content.replace(OLD, NEW, 1))

print("✅ Window recovery added to onStartCommand — no service restart needed")
EOF

3:24 AM
Two separate bugs. Let me address both.

Bug 1 — Pill always showing regardless of toggle The window recovery code in onStartCommand calls showWidget() unconditionally when !isViewAdded, even when the toggle is off. It doesn't check toggleEnabled.

Bug 2 — Toggle not saving The toggle saves to giglens_ui SharedPreferences with key floating_button_enabled, but something is reading/writing it inconsistently. Let me see the Settings toggle code:

    }

    // CORRECT: request from Settings when driver enables floating button — one step
    // WRONG:   requiring driver to go to MainActivity separately to grant permission

        androidx.activity.result.contract.ActivityResultContracts.StartActivityForResult()
    ) { result ->
        if (result.resultCode == android.app.Activity.RESULT_OK && result.data != null) {
            val serviceIntent = android.content.Intent(this, com.augusteenterprise.giglens.service.ScreenCaptureService::class.java).apply {
                putExtra(com.augusteenterprise.giglens.service.ScreenCaptureService.EXTRA_RESULT_CODE, result.resultCode)
                putExtra(com.augusteenterprise.giglens.service.ScreenCaptureService.EXTRA_RESULT_DATA, result.data)
            }
            if (android.os.Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
                startForegroundService(serviceIntent)
            } else {
                startService(serviceIntent)
            }
            if (!CaptureButtonService.isRunning) {
                startService(android.content.Intent(this, CaptureButtonService::class.java))
            }
            android.widget.Toast.makeText(this, "Screen capture enabled", android.widget.Toast.LENGTH_SHORT).show()
        } else {
            android.widget.Toast.makeText(this, "Screen capture permission denied", android.widget.Toast.LENGTH_SHORT).show()
        }
    }

        super.onResume()

        // CORRECT: re-evaluate when returning from system Accessibility settings screen
        // WRONG:   only checking on onCreate — misses state changes made in system settings
        updateAccessibilityPermUI()
    }

OverlayPermissionHelper.requestPermission(this)
                    return@setOnCheckedChangeListener
                }
                startWidgetService()
                GlobalScope.launch {
                    GigLensApp.instance.database.appConfigDao()
                        .setValue(AppConfigKeys.WIDGET_ENABLED, "true")
                    Log.d(TAG, "widget_enabled saved: true")
                }

                stopService(Intent(this, OfferOverlayService::class.java))
                GlobalScope.launch {
                    GigLensApp.instance.database.appConfigDao()
                        .setValue(AppConfigKeys.WIDGET_ENABLED, "false")
                    Log.d(TAG, "widget_enabled saved: false")

            updateAccessibilityPermUI()

            startActivity(android.content.Intent(Settings.ACTION_ACCESSIBILITY_SETTINGS))

            val intent = android.content.Intent(android.content.Intent.ACTION_VIEW,
                android.net.Uri.parse("https://www.fueleconomy.gov/feg/Find.do"))

            val costPerMile = scorerDao.getValue(ScorerConfigKeys.COST_PER_MILE) ?: 0.90

            val resultDuration = scorerDao.getValue(ScorerConfigKeys.RESULT_DISPLAY_SECONDS) ?: 60.0
            val mpg = scorerDao.getValue(ScorerConfigKeys.MPG) ?: 30.0
            val gasPrice = scorerDao.getValue(ScorerConfigKeys.GAS_PRICE) ?: 3.20
            val dataSharing = appDao.getValue(AppConfigKeys.DATA_SHARING) ?: "none"
            val widgetEnabled = appDao.getValue(AppConfigKeys.WIDGET_ENABLED) == "true"
            val captureMode = appDao.getValue(AppConfigKeys.AUTO_CAPTURE_MODE) ?: "off"

3:27 AM
Now I have the full picture. SettingsActivity saves to AppConfigKeys.WIDGET_ENABLED in the DB, but MainActivity.onResume reads from SharedPreferences("giglens_ui", "floating_button_enabled"). They're using two completely different storage mechanisms — that's the mismatch.

The fix: make MainActivity.onResume read from the DB like Settings does, OR make Settings also write to SharedPreferences. The DB approach is correct per your standing rules (no hardcoded values, DB is source of truth).

Let me see how MainActivity currently reads AppConfigKeys.WIDGET_ENABLED:

bash

3:28 AM
Clear. WIDGET_ENABLED in the DB is the source of truth everywhere except onResume which reads SharedPreferences. Let me see the full onResume block to write the patch:

bash
sed -n '195,230p' /home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/ui/MainActivity.kt
override fun onResume() {
        super.onResume()
        // CORRECT: start overlay service while app is in foreground — avoids Android 12+ bg restriction
        // WRONG:   starting from ScreenCaptureService (background) — throws ForegroundServiceStartNotAllowedException
        // CORRECT: start overlay as soon as overlay permission is granted — pill should
        //          always be visible when toggle is ON, independent of screen capture state
        // WRONG:   gating on ScreenCaptureService.isRunning — pill never shows until DoorDash opens
        val prefs = getSharedPreferences("giglens_ui", MODE_PRIVATE)
        val toggleEnabled = prefs.getBoolean("floating_button_enabled", false)
        val canDraw = android.provider.Settings.canDrawOverlays(this)
        Log.d("MainActivity", "onResume: isRunning=${OfferOverlayService.isRunning} toggleEnabled=$toggleEnabled canDraw=$canDraw")
        if (!OfferOverlayService.isRunning && toggleEnabled && canDraw) {
            Log.d("MainActivity", "onResume: starting OfferOverlayService")
            val overlayIntent = Intent(this, OfferOverlayService::class.java)
            // CORRECT: always startForegroundService on Android O+ — service calls startForeground() in onCreate()
            // WRONG: startService on Android 12+ — OS kills it before onStartCommand fires on Android 16
            if (android.os.Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
                startForegroundService(overlayIntent)
            } else {
                startService(overlayIntent)
            }
        }
        // Handle restart_capture flag from CAPTURE_DEAD pill tap
        // CORRECT: re-request MediaProjection when driver taps the warning pill
        // WRONG: requiring driver to navigate manually — too many steps mid-shift
        if (intent?.getBooleanExtra("restart_capture", false) == true) {
            Log.d("MainActivity", "restart_capture flag set — re-requesting MediaProjection")
            intent.removeExtra("restart_capture")
            if (!ScreenCaptureService.isRunning) {
                showAutoModeDialog()
            }
        }
        // CORRECT: if returning from accessibility settings and it's now enabled,

Show more
3:28 AM
Got it. Here's the patch — switches onResume from SharedPreferences to DB, runs on a coroutine since DB reads are async:

bash
python3 << 'EOF'
FILE = "/home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/ui/MainActivity.kt"

OLD = '''        // CORRECT: start overlay service while app is in foreground — avoids Android 12+ bg restriction
        // WRONG:   starting from ScreenCaptureService (background) — throws ForegroundServiceStartNotAllowedException
        // CORRECT: start overlay as soon as overlay permission is granted — pill should
        //          always be visible when toggle is ON, independent of screen capture state
        // WRONG:   gating on ScreenCaptureService.isRunning — pill never shows until DoorDash opens
        val prefs = getSharedPreferences("giglens_ui", MODE_PRIVATE)
        val toggleEnabled = prefs.getBoolean("floating_button_enabled", false)
        val canDraw = android.provider.Settings.canDrawOverlays(this)
        Log.d("MainActivity", "onResume: isRunning=${OfferOverlayService.isRunning} toggleEnabled=$toggleEnabled canDraw=$canDraw")
        if (!OfferOverlayService.isRunning && toggleEnabled && canDraw) {
            Log.d("MainActivity", "onResume: starting OfferOverlayService")
            val overlayIntent = Intent(this, OfferOverlayService::class.java)
            // CORRECT: always startForegroundService on Android O+ — service calls startForeground() in onCreate()
            // WRONG: startService on Android 12+ — OS kills it before onStartCommand fires on Android 16
            if (android.os.Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
                startForegroundService(overlayIntent)
            } else {
                startService(overlayIntent)
            }
        }'''

NEW = '''        // CORRECT: read WIDGET_ENABLED from DB — single source of truth shared with SettingsActivity
        // WRONG: reading from SharedPreferences("giglens_ui") — SettingsActivity writes to DB, not prefs
        val canDraw = android.provider.Settings.canDrawOverlays(this)
        lifecycleScope.launch {
            val toggleEnabled = GigLensApp.instance.database.appConfigDao()
                .getValue(AppConfigKeys.WIDGET_ENABLED) == "true"
            Log.d("MainActivity", "onResume: isRunning=${OfferOverlayService.isRunning} toggleEnabled=$toggleEnabled canDraw=$canDraw")
            if (!OfferOverlayService.isRunning && toggleEnabled && canDraw) {
                Log.d("MainActivity", "onResume: starting OfferOverlayService")
                val overlayIntent = Intent(this@MainActivity, OfferOverlayService::class.java)
                // CORRECT: always startForegroundService on Android O+ — service calls startForeground() in onCreate()
                // WRONG: startService on Android 12+ — OS kills it before onStartCommand fires on Android 16
                if (android.os.Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
                    startForegroundService(overlayIntent)
                } else {
                    startService(overlayIntent)
                }
            }
        }'''

with open(FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    content = f.read()

if OLD not in content:
    print("❌ Anchor not found — aborting.")
    exit(1)

with open(FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    f.write(content.replace(OLD, NEW, 1))

print("✅ onResume patched — WIDGET_ENABLED now read from DB")
EOF
Now fix the window recovery in OfferOverlayService to also check the DB toggle before showing the widget:

bash
python3 << 'EOF'
FILE = "/home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/service/OfferOverlayService.kt"

OLD = '''        // CORRECT: verify overlay window is still attached on every onStartCommand — re-add silently if lost
        // WRONG: assuming isViewAdded means window is valid — process restart loses window without clearing flag
        if (isViewAdded && rootView != null && layoutParams != null) {
            try {
                windowManager.updateViewLayout(rootView!!, layoutParams!!)
            } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
                Log.w(TAG, "Overlay window lost — re-adding silently: ${e.message}")
                isViewAdded = false
                showWidget()
            }
        } else if (!isViewAdded) {
            showWidget()
        }'''

NEW = '''        // CORRECT: verify overlay window is still attached on every onStartCommand — re-add silently if lost
        // WRONG: assuming isViewAdded means window is valid — process restart loses window without clearing flag
        // CORRECT: check WIDGET_ENABLED before re-adding — don't show pill if toggle is off
        // WRONG: calling showWidget() unconditionally — pill appears even when driver disabled it
        val widgetEnabled = runBlocking {
            GigLensApp.instance.database.appConfigDao()
                .getValue(com.augusteenterprise.giglens.data.AppConfigKeys.WIDGET_ENABLED) == "true"
        }
        if (widgetEnabled) {
            if (isViewAdded && rootView != null && layoutParams != null) {
                try {
                    windowManager.updateViewLayout(rootView!!, layoutParams!!)
                } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
                    Log.w(TAG, "Overlay window lost — re-adding silently: ${e.message}")
                    isViewAdded = false
                    showWidget()
                }
            } else if (!isViewAdded) {
                showWidget()
            }
        }'''

with open(FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    content = f.read()

if OLD not in content:
    print("❌ Anchor not found — aborting.")
    exit(1)

with open(FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    f.write(content.replace(OLD, NEW, 1))

print("✅ Window recovery now gated on WIDGET_ENABLED DB value")
EOF
Then build and install:

Switch to DoorDash, wait for live offer
Confirm 📷 pill appears

bash
grep -n "btnSaveSettings\|saveSettings\|WIDGET_ENABLED" /home/poppa/giglens/app/src/main/java/com/augusteenterprise/giglens/ui/SettingsActivity.kt | head -20
The toggle should save immediately via the setOnCheckedChangeListener without needing the Save button — that's how it's coded. But if saveSettings() also writes WIDGET_ENABLED, hitting Save could overwrite the toggle state.

Paste the output and we'll confirm. Then go test Test 3 — that's the critical one.
